columns.name: signal
columns.description: inline quality reading taken during the run

columns.name: outcome
columns.description: final quality score (higher = better; below 15 = rejected)

columns.name: pressure
columns.description: line pressure setting logged for the run

columns.name: flow_rate
columns.description: coolant flow logged for the run

columns.name: rpm
columns.description: spindle speed logged for the run

columns.name: hall_rh
columns.description: hall relative humidity at the run

columns.name: vibration
columns.description: machine vibration level during the run

columns.name: noise_db
columns.description: hall noise level during the run

columns.name: wear_index
columns.description: tooling wear index at the run

columns.name: supply_age
columns.description: age of the consumables lot in service

stakes.narrative: A process line must set one intensity per campaign and price its reject exposure. The line logs NINETEEN signals per unit -- settings, material readings, hall conditions, maintenance counters -- and its historical records were taken in batches over an era in which BOTH the meter's calibration and the operating intensity drifted. Most of what is logged is a bystander; the paying question is unchanged: what does a deliberately set intensity really do to the reject rate.

functionals.name: exceedance
functionals.brief_clause: a unit whose quality lands below the reject line of 15 is scrapped at the line's cost

shift.description: hall/period baseline (a property of when the run happens; targetable in experiments, not set per-unit)

control_surface.experiment_note: experimental readings are taken by the SAME instrument in fresh runs: they arrive in batches too (batch_id column), each batch with its own meter state
control_surface.deliverable_note: model(regime, n, seed) -> table over [driver, signal, outcome] (no batch_id: batches are a property of the measurement runs, not of the process). Model the PROCESS, not the meter: observed readings include per-batch meter offsets and instrument noise; your model should reproduce true quality.
